'''Michael Sio''' (born 16 May 1993) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who was most recently contracted to [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. He primarily plays as a {{rlp|hk}}, and has represented [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]], [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] and [[Pacific Rugby League International#Polynesian Cup|2015 Polynesian Cup]].

==Early life==
Sio was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand.

He attended [[St. Paul's College, Auckland|St. Paul's College]], where he was a prefect.<ref>[http://www.stuff.co.nz/auckland/local-news/auckland-city-harbour-news/4832740/League-player-balances-books-and-ball League player balances books and ball] ''stuff.co.nz'', 1 April 2011</ref>

==Playing career==
Sio played junior league for the [[Marist Saints]] before he was signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]]. He played for the Junior Warriors in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Holden Cup]], captaining the side in 2013. At the end of the 2013 season he was awarded the [[Sonny Fai]] Medal as the Junior Warriors' player of the year.<ref>[http://www.warriors.co.nz/news-display/Vodafone%20Player%20of%20the%20Year%20Michael%20Sio/84719 Vodafone Player of the Year: Michael Sio] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131029205529/http://www.warriors.co.nz/news-display/Vodafone%20Player%20of%20the%20Year%20Michael%20Sio/84719 |date=2013-10-29}} ''warriors.co.nz'', 19 September 2013</ref>

He played for the [[New Zealand Residents]] in 2011.<ref>[http://www.warriors.co.nz/nycplayerprofiledisplay/Warriors-NYC/Michael%20Sio/5808 Michael Sio] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131029210442/http://www.warriors.co.nz/nycplayerprofiledisplay/Warriors-NYC/Michael%20Sio/5808 |date=2013-10-29}} ''warriors.co.nz''</ref> Sio was called up to the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]] to replace [[Masada Iosefa]].<ref>[http://rleague.com/news/Sio-called-into-Samoa-squad-to-replace-injured-Iosefa_126236/ Sio called into Samoa squad to replace injured Iosefa] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131029202642/http://rleague.com/news/Sio-called-into-Samoa-squad-to-replace-injured-Iosefa_126236/ |date=2013-10-29}} ''rleague.com'',25 October 2013</ref>

In May 2014, Michael played for Samoa in the [[Pacific Rugby League International#2014 test|2014 Pacific International]]. On 7 October 2014, Sio was selected in the Samoan side for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] series. He came in as a late replacement to play in the [[Pacific Rugby League International#Polynesian Cup|2015 Polynesian Cup]] test-match.

Sio joined the [[Mackay Cutters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] for the 2015 season.<ref>[http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/former-warriors-duo-join-cutters/ Former Warriors duo join Cutters] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141204163015/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/former-warriors-duo-join-cutters/ |date=4 December 2014 }} ''rugbyleagueweek.com.au'', 27 November 2014</ref> However, on 22 June 2015, he signed a {{frac|1|1|2}}-year contract with [[Super League]] club [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.wakefieldwildcats.co.uk/news/2015/06/wildcats-add-sio-to-squad |title=News | WILDCATS ADD SIO TO SQUAD |access-date=23 June 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150623145345/http://www.wakefieldwildcats.co.uk/news/2015/06/wildcats-add-sio-to-squad |archive-date=23 June 2015 |url-status=dead }}</ref> He left the club at the end of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/42158273|title=Michael Sio: Wakefield Trinity hooker leaves Super League club|publisher=BBC Sport|date=28 November 2017|accessdate=24 January 2018}}</ref>
